text: I'm so sorry you're going through this. It sounds like you're trying to understand what's happening with a breast lump and the steps to diagnose and treat it. Don't worry, I'm here to help. Cancer is a disease where abnormal cells in our body grow and multiply uncontrollably, causing harm to the surrounding tissues and organs. In the case of breast cancer, it starts in the breast tissue and can spread to other parts of the body if not treated. It's more common in women, especially as they get older, and can be influenced by factors like family history and lifestyle. If you're concerned about breast cancer, it's essential to talk to a doctor who can perform a physical examination and recommend further tests if needed. Remember, with early detection and treatment, many people with breast cancer can recover and live healthy lives. You're not alone, and there's always hope and support available, so don't hesitate to reach out to a medical professional if you have any concerns.
